questions.question: Calculate the height of a cylinder that has a volume of $500\text{ cm}^3$ and a base radius of $8\text{ cm}$. Give your answer correct to 3 significant figures.
questions.explanation:
$V = \pi r^2 h \Rightarrow 500 = \pi \times 8^2 \times h \Rightarrow 500 = 64\pi h$2$h = 500 / (64\pi) \approx 2.486...$, which rounds to $2.49$.

**Common mistake:** A student might divide the volume by the radius only once or forget to include $\pi$ in the division.

questions.question: Find the total surface area of a solid cone with a radius of $4\text{ cm}$ and a vertical height of $3\text{ cm}$. (Use $\pi = 3.142$)
questions.explanation:
First find slant height $l = \sqrt{3^2 + 4^2} = 5$. Curved area $= \pi rl = \pi(4)(5) = 20\pi$2$= \pi r^2 = 16\pi$2$= 36\pi \approx 113.1$.

**Common mistake:** The student might use the vertical height ($3$) in the surface area formula instead of the slant height ($5$).

questions.question: A square-based pyramid has a base side of $5\text{ m}$ and a vertical height of $4\text{ m}$. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation:
Volume $= \frac{1}{3} \times \text{base area} \times \text{height} = \frac{1}{3} \times (5 \times 5) \times 4 = \frac{100}{3} \approx 33.3$.

**Common mistake:** The student might multiply base area by height and forget the one-third factor.

questions.question: Determine the total surface area of a solid hemisphere with a radius of $7\text{ cm}$ in terms of $\pi$.
questions.explanation:
Curved area $= 2\pi r^2 = 98\pi$2$= \pi r^2 = 49\pi$2$= 147\pi$.

**Common mistake:** A student might only calculate the curved surface area ($98\pi$) and forget to add the circular base.

questions.question: If $3$ litres of oil are removed from an upright cylindrical can, the level falls by $10\text{ cm}$. Find the radius of the can in centimetres ($1\text{ litre} = 1000\text{ cm}^3$).
questions.explanation:
Volume removed $= 3000\text{ cm}^3$. $\pi r^2 h = 3000 \Rightarrow \pi r^2 (10) = 3000 \Rightarrow r^2 = 300 / \pi \approx 95.49$. $r = \sqrt{95.49} \approx 9.77$.

**Common mistake:** The student might fail to convert litres to $\text{cm}^3$ correctly, using $3$ instead of $3000$.
